name: Bola del Mundo Loop from Cercedilla
name_original: Las Siete Revueltas desde Cerdedillla — circular por el Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama
description: Conquer the Bola del Mundo Loop from Cercedilla, a 25.5-mile touring cycling route with 4128 feet of elevation gain and panoramic views.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Cercedilla traverse diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. The region features extensive Scots pine forests, granite and gneiss geology, and prominent peaks like Siete Picos and La Peñota. The Fuenfría Valley is a notable natural feature, characterized by mountain streams and varied wildlife. Roads in the Sierra de Guadarrama are generally quiet and well-maintained, offering both demanding ascents and thrilling descents. Best touring cycling routes around Cercedilla The most popular touring cycling route is Bola del Mundo Loop from Cercedilla, a 25.5 miles (41.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 34 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and panoramic views of the surrounding mountains. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Majavilán Stream Spring – Queen's Viewpoint loop from Cercedilla, a difficult 16.6 miles (26.7 km) path. This route winds through dense pine forests and offers rewarding vistas of the Fuenfría Valley. Local touring cyclists also love the Fuente de la Reina – Queen's Viewpoint loop from Antiguo Apeadero de Cercedilla Pueblo, a 25.0 miles (40.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant climbs, often completed in about 3 hours 28 minutes. Touring cycling around Cercedilla is defined by extensive Scots pine forests, granite mountain passes, and scenic valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from challenging climbs to more moderate paths. The routes in Cercedilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cercedilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Cercedilla?
A: Touring cycling routes in Cercedilla primarily cater to experienced cyclists. Out of the 18 available routes, 15 are rated as difficult, featuring significant elevation gains and challenging terrain. There are 3 moderate routes, but no easy options for touring cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Cercedilla?
A: The routes in Cercedilla traverse diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. You can expect extensive Scots pine forests, granite and gneiss geology, and prominent peaks. Roads are generally quiet and well-maintained, offering both demanding ascents and thrilling descents. Some segments may be unpaved and more technical, particularly for mountain biking, though the touring routes focus on roads.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Cercedilla?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Cercedilla are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1190712194/bola-del-mundo-loop-from-cercedilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bola del Mundo Loop from Cercedilla&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38163708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Majavilán Stream Spring – Queen's Viewpoint loop from Cercedilla&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options, allowing you to start and end in the same location.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling in Cercedilla?
A: Cercedilla offers stunning natural beauty and historical landmarks. You can cycle past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/641391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Road of Puerto de la Fuenfría&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of an ancient route. Other notable features include the Fuenfría Valley, dense Scots pine forests, and views of prominent peaks like Siete Picos. The region also features natural pools fed by mountain waters in summer.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Cercedilla?
A: Cercedilla experiences a humid continental Mediterranean climate. Mild summers are ideal for cycling, offering pleasant temperatures. Autumn and spring also provide excellent conditions with cooler weather and beautiful scenery. While winter offers opportunities for skiing nearby, touring cycling might be more challenging due to colder temperatures and potential snow at higher elevations.

Q: Can I reach Cercedilla by public transport with my bike?
A: Yes, Cercedilla is well-connected by public transport. It's located just an hour from Madrid, and its train station is the highest in the Guadarrama range, making it a convenient gateway from the city to the mountains. It is generally possible to transport bikes on regional trains, but it's advisable to check the specific train operator's policy and schedules for bike carriage before your trip.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about touring cycling in Cercedilla?
A: The touring cycling routes in Cercedilla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, thrilling descents, and the stunning alpine scenery, dense forests, and high mountain landscapes within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park.

Q: Are there any very long touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders looking for longer distances, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31559972" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontón Alto Dam – Segovia Bike Path loop from Antiguo Apeadero de Cercedilla Pueblo&lt;/a&gt; offer significant mileage. This difficult route spans nearly 80 km (50 miles) with over 1700 meters of elevation gain, providing a substantial challenge.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of reservoirs or lakes?
A: Yes, some routes provide views of water bodies. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31559972" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontón Alto Dam – Segovia Bike Path loop from Antiguo Apeadero de Cercedilla Pueblo&lt;/a&gt; passes by the Pontón Alto Dam. Additionally, nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4559864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Air Force Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4560049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navacerrada Reservoir Dam&lt;/a&gt;, which might be visible from certain cycling paths or accessible as detours.

Q: What are some of the highest points or passes accessible by touring bike in Cercedilla?
A: Cercedilla is known for its mountain passes. While cycling, you can experience routes that climb towards well-known passes like Puerto de la Fuenfría and Puerto de Navacerrada. These passes offer significant elevation and panoramic views, often featured in professional cycling events.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Fuenfría Valley?
A: Yes, the Fuenfría Valley is a significant natural feature in Cercedilla, known for its diverse wildlife and mountain streams. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38163708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Majavilán Stream Spring – Queen's Viewpoint loop from Cercedilla&lt;/a&gt; winds through dense pine forests and offers rewarding vistas of the Fuenfría Valley, allowing cyclists to immerse themselves in this beautiful area.
tips: Perhaps the most beautiful of those we find on the ascent to Fuenfría, receives its name from the poetic contests that Luis Rosales held there.
It is an emblematic viewpoint of the Fuenfría valley, located on the Republic road and about 800 meters from the port.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle the Bola del Mundo Loop from Cercedilla?
faqs.answer: This challenging touring bicycle route typically takes around 3 hours and 34 minutes to complete. However, this is an estimated moving time; actual duration will vary based on your fitness level, stops for views, and the demanding terrain.

faqs.question: Is the Bola del Mundo Loop from Cercedilla suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and is exceedingly challenging, considered one of the toughest mountain passes in Madrid. It features significant elevation gain, extreme gradients up to 30%, and a very rough concrete surface on the final ascent. It requires a high level of physical fitness, endurance, and technical bike handling skills.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Bola del Mundo Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse and demanding. You'll encounter forest tracks, which can have roots and loose stones, especially on sections like the Camino del Calvario. As you ascend, surfaces vary between paved roads and more rugged paths. The final 3.5 to 4 kilometers to the Bola del Mundo summit are particularly infamous, consisting of a grooved, crumbling concrete track with breaks, potholes, and a bumpy surface that makes maintaining traction difficult.

faqs.question: What scenic viewpoints or landmarks can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular panoramic views of the Sierra de Guadarrama. Key viewpoints include the Poets' Viewpoint and the Queen's Viewpoint. The summit itself, marked by distinctive red and white telecommunications antennas, provides unparalleled vistas of both the Madrid and Segovia sides of the range.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Bola del Mundo Loop from Cercedilla?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this loop is typically from late spring through early autumn (May to October) when the weather is more stable and the higher elevations are free of snow. Winter cycling is possible but can be very challenging due to snow, ice, and extreme cold, especially at the summit.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting the Bola del Mundo Loop from Cercedilla?
faqs.answer: Cercedilla offers several parking options, including areas near the train station, which is a common starting point for routes in the area. It's advisable to arrive early, especially on weekends, as parking can fill up quickly.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on established trails within the Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama. However, it's crucial to respect park regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of environmental protection rules.

faqs.question: Is the Bola del Mundo Loop from Cercedilla dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama, they must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Given the extreme difficulty and length of this particular cycling route, it may not be suitable for all dogs.

faqs.question: Does the Bola del Mundo Loop intersect with other notable trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several other well-known paths. You'll encounter parts of the Madrid Way of Saint James, Valle de la Fuenfría, and the Ruta Verde Puerto de Navacerrada - Cercedilla, among others.

faqs.question: What makes the Bola del Mundo particularly famous for cyclists?
faqs.answer: The Bola del Mundo is an iconic high-altitude climb in the Sierra de Guadarrama, revered for its extreme challenge and stunning views. It gained significant fame by being featured multiple times as a stage finish in the prestigious Vuelta a España professional cycling race, cementing its status as a legendary ascent.

faqs.question: What is the most challenging part of the Bola del Mundo Loop?
faqs.answer: The most challenging part is undoubtedly the final 3.5 to 4 kilometers to the Bola del Mundo summit. This section features average gradients of 12-12.5% with ramps as high as 18-19%, and even up to 30% in some parts. The crumbling, grooved concrete surface adds a significant technical challenge to the already brutal climb.
